item.title: What did jury decide in social media case against Meta and Google?
item.description: A Los Angeles jury found Alphabet's Google and Meta liable on Wednesday in a landmark ​youth social media addiction trial.

item.title: KKR to acquire Nothing Bundt Cakes for over $2 billion, WSJ reports
item.description: U.S. private ​equity firm ‌KKR has struck ​a ​deal to acquire ⁠bakery ​chain ​Nothing Bundt Cakes from ​Roark ​Capital for over $2 ‌billion, ⁠including debt, the Wall ​Street ​Journal ⁠reported on ​Wednesday.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp, Jindal steel sale talks falter on pension, energy costs, sources say
item.description: Discussions of a possible sale of Thyssenkrupp's steel unit to Jindal Steel International could be called off due to differences over pension liabilities, ​investments and energy costs, four people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Infosys to acquire US firms Optimum Healthcare IT for $465 million, Stratus for $95 million
item.description: Indian IT services firm ​Infosys said on ‌Wednesday it had entered into ​separate agreements ​to acquire U.S.-based ⁠technology companies ​Optimum Healthcare IT ​and Stratus in all-cash deals.

item.title: JetBlue taps advisers for potential sale, Semafor reports
item.description: JetBlue Airways has brought in advisers to assess the viability of a sale to a rival carrier, Semafor reported on Wednesday, citing ​people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US suspends anti-smog fuel rules in bid to ease pump prices
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration announced on Wednesday it will temporarily suspend federal anti-smog ​regulations on seasonal gasoline blends to combat higher pump prices ‌since the start of the war on Iran.

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial
item.description: A Los Angeles jury found Alphabet's ‌Google and Meta liable on Wednesday in a landmark social media addiction lawsuit.

item.title: NYSE American Options resolves technical issue that hit trading, market data
item.description: Intercontinental Exchange's NYSE ⁠American Options ​said ​on Wednesday it has resolved a technical ​issue that ​had affected trading, ‌post-trade ⁠and market data.

item.title: SpaceX IPO leaves some private share buyers unsure what they own
item.description: Entrepreneur Tejpaul Bhatia is confident he owns a slice of Elon Musk’s SpaceX. But he can't be 100% sure.

item.title: Fed reports narrowing $19.6 billion loss for operations in 2025
item.description: The Federal Reserve reported ​a narrower loss last year ‌in an audited financial statement released on Wednesday.

item.title: Lyft rolls out driver-relief program as US fuel prices climb
item.description: Ride-hailing company Lyft said on Wednesday it will roll out a temporary driver-relief program ​in the United States, as higher gasoline ‌prices squeeze earnings for gig workers.

item.title: Legal software firm Harvey valued at $11 billion in latest funding round
item.description: Legal software firm Harvey has raised $200 million at an $11 billion valuation and will use some of ​the funds to expand its artificial intelligence agent ‌as investor appetite for AI tools accelerates.

item.title: US Supreme Court backs Cox in fight over pirated music
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled on Wednesday Cox Communications cannot be held liable for piracy by its internet service subscribers of songs owned by Sony Music , Warner Music ​Group , Universal Music Group and other labels, ending their billion-dollar-plus music copyright lawsuit.

item.title: Meta to lay off hundreds of employees, The Information reports
item.description: Meta Platforms will lay off a few hundred people across its ​Reality Labs division, social media teams and ‌recruiting operations on Wednesday, The Information reported, citing two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Democratic lawmaker Warren presses Fed official over conflicts
item.description: U.S. Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren ​on Wednesday pressed top Federal ‌Reserve official and former veteran Wall Street bank attorney Randall Guynn to ​explain how he is ​handling potential conflicts of interest in ⁠his new role, according to ​a letter reviewed by Reuters.

item.title: Beyond Meat delays fourth-quarter results
item.description: Beyond Meat said on Wednesday it was delaying the ​reporting of its fourth-quarter results ‌by about a week as it needs additional time to complete some ​internal processes.

item.title: Can the US government be held liable for the LaGuardia collision?
item.description: ​A look at the potential liability of federal and local authorities.

item.title: US current account deficit contracts sharply in the fourth quarter
item.description: The U.S. current account deficit narrowed sharply in the fourth quarter, hitting the lowest level in nearly five years, amid a rise in primary income and ​a reduction in the goods trade deficit partly because of tariffs ‌on imports.

item.title: Albemarle starts environmental review process for lithium extraction project in Chile
item.description: Albemarle said on Wednesday that it ​has started the environmental review ‌process for its first project through Direct Lithium Extraction (DLE) in Chile.

item.title: Trump names Jensen Huang, Mark Zuckerberg to 13-member tech council
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump plans to appoint Meta ​Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Oracle Executive ‌Chairman Larry Ellison and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to a council that ​weighs in on AI policy ​and other issues, the White ⁠House said on Wednesday.

item.title: SLB expands Nvidia partnership to develop AI infrastructure for energy sector
item.description: Oilfield services company SLB said on Wednesday it would expand its partnership with Nvidia to develop ​artificial intelligence infrastructure and models for the energy ‌industry, as companies look to scale up use of the technology.

item.title: Six Flags appoints new chair amid call for sale
item.description: Theme park operator Six Flags Entertainment ‌on Wednesday announced a change in leadership on its board, naming Richard Haddrill as executive chairman.

item.title: BlackRock CEO Fink warns of 'global recession' if oil goes to $150, BBC reports
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said oil prices could reach $150 a ​barrel and cause a "global recession" if Iran "remains ‌a threat" even after the war ends.

item.title: US import prices post largest gain in nearly four years in February
item.description: U.S. import prices increased by the most in nearly four years in February as energy costs surged in ​anticipation of conflict in the Middle East, adding ‌to signs that inflation is poised to accelerate in the months ahead.

item.title: AI boom accelerates China's chip industry growth as demand strains supply chain
item.description: China's chip industry is showing strong growth momentum as a global sprint to build AI infrastructure creates ​an explosion in demand, sparking higher capital spending and capacity expansion as chipmakers race to keep ‌up, executives said on Wednesday.

item.title: Google to label verified investment apps in India amid scam crackdown
item.description: Alphabets's Google will label verified ​investment apps on ‌its app store in India, a move ​aimed at helping ​users spot legitimate trading ⁠platforms and ​avoid scams, a senior ​official at the country's markets regulator Securities and ​Exchange Board ​of India said on Wednesday.

item.title: US company CFOs saw a solid economic outlook, at least until war broke out, Fed survey shows
item.description: U.S. corporate finance chiefs' outlook for the economy improved over the first months of the year, at least until the outbreak of ​the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, with executives expecting to increase employment ‌amid solid revenue growth, though with continued pressure as well to raise prices, according to a Federal Reserve survey.

item.title: AmEx deepens push into small and mid-market businesses with new cards
item.description: American ​Express launched a new commercial credit card on Wednesday and said ‌it plans to introduce another later this year, as it aims to solidify its foothold among small- and middle-market companies.

item.title: German army eyes AI tools to expedite wartime decision-making
item.description: The German army is working to ‌accelerate wartime decision-making through artificial intelligence tools capable of analysing battlefield data more rapidly than humans, drawing lessons from Ukrainian and other forces, its commander told Reuters.

item.title: Morning Bid: Fifteen points to ponder
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today
